phase: PLAN.md S2.3 — corrected unified mechanism finding
correction_note: With clean same-offset comparison (slot 0 has same prompt in both NP=2 and NP=4 captures), the FA and MMQ kernel-internal nondeterminism are revealed as different visibility surfaces of the SAME underlying mechanism, not two distinct mechanisms.

unified_mechanism.name: fp_arithmetic_internal_batch_shape_sensitivity
unified_mechanism.description: The same kernel TEMPLATE running on slot-K's IDENTICAL inputs produces fp32-epsilon-level numerical variation across different batch widths (NP=2 vs NP=4). This is data-dependent: at most layer/prompt combinations the noise stays below F16 precision and the F16-stored residual is byte-identical. At some combinations (data-dependent), the FA kernel's noise already crosses F16 at its output (p1/p3/p4 layer 3). At other combinations, FA stays sub-F16 but the FFN MMQ kernels amplify the noise above F16 (p0 layer 19).
unified_mechanism.fp32_eps_noise_floor: approximately 1e-7 at any FA call with the same template at different NP; this is the floor that propagates
unified_mechanism.f16_precision_visibility_threshold: ~1e-3 to 1e-4 at residual magnitudes — depends on layer position and data

open_invariants_now_partially_closed.Open_FA_SourceClass_OtherPrompts: p2 at layer 3 = NO drift (CORRECTION). p4 at layer 3 = drift confirmed. p5 cannot be tested cleanly (np=4 offset>=5 requires p8.txt which doesn't exist).
open_invariants_now_partially_closed.Open_P0_EarlyFALayersByteIdentical: p0 layer 3 confirmed byte-identical at l_out level (FA noise sub-F16); the pattern strongly suggests layers 7, 11, 15 also byte-identical but not yet verified.
open_invariants_now_partially_closed.still_open_FA_internal_mechanism: The specific COMPUTATION inside wmma_f16 that introduces fp32-eps batch-shape-dependent variation. Needs kernel-internal reading. Likely candidates: parallel_blocks reduction at parallel_blocks > 1, cross-warp __syncthreads serialization order, KQ_acc warp-shuffle ordering, or atomic-fragment combining when ntiles_x = 1 but ne11 varies.
open_invariants_now_partially_closed.still_open_MMQ_internal_mechanism: Same as above for MMQ. Likely: stream-K partial-sum combining or per-block fragment reduction order when ne11 varies and ntiles_x = 1.

implication_for_stage_2_design: Stage 2 fix scope = ONE underlying problem (kernel fp-arithmetic-internal batch-shape sensitivity) addressed by applying TML batch-invariance recipe to BOTH the FA wmma_f16 kernel AND the MMQ kernel. The 'two distinct mechanisms' framing was premature — they share the same root cause.
